content.full_text:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Since your question does not pertain to a particular
governmental decision, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2
Fnrn Polrrrcal Pnncrtcps C ouvttssloN
P.O. Box 807 . "lllt J Strc'et . Sacramento. CA 95S12-0lt()7
(916) ill--s660' Fax (916) i12-0tttJ6
January 23,2001
Susan Wallace, Executive Officer
State of California
Youthful Offender Parole Board
10291Street, Suite 500
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. l-00-277
Dear Ms. Wallace:
QUESTION
You have asked whether serving on the Board of Directors of Family for
Children (FFC), a non-profit orgarization which places children in foster care and
provides adoption remedies, will present any conflicts of interest with regard to your
position as Executive Officer of the Youthful Offender Parole Board (YOPB).
CONCLUSION
Since FFC is a non-profit organization, it is not a business entity as contemplated
in the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act. Additionally, since your service on the
FFC Board of Directors is unpaid, you do not have an economic interest in the non-profit
organization as a source of income. Since you have no economic interests, there is no
potential for a financial conflict of interest as contemplated by the Act. The question of
whether your service on the FFC Board is incompatible with your position as Executive
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83 I 14; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


File No. I-00-277
Page No. 2
Officer of the YOPB may be best answered by consulting Government Code Section
lggg} or other relevant statutes, your agency's Statement of lncompatible Activities and
your general counsel. The Commission is not authorized to advise you regarding laws
outside the Act.
FACTS
You were appointed Executive Officer of the State of Califomia Youthful
Offender Parole Board in Febuary of 2000. You have recently been asked to serve on
the Board of Directors of Family For Children, a non-profit organization based in
Sacramento which places children in foster care and provides adoption remedies. You
will be unpaid and your commitments to the FFC Board will be fulfrlled in the evening
hours.
DISCUSSION
The Act prohibits a public official from making, participating in making, or
attempting to use his or her official position to influence a govemmental decision in
which he or she has a financial interest. Section 87103 states that a public official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect on the official, a member of his or her immediate family, or on:
. 
Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investmenf
of $2,000 or more (Section 87103(a); Regulation 18703.1(a)); or in which he or
she is a director, oficer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any posttion of
managemenr (Section 87 1 03(d); Regulation 1 8703. I (b));
. 
Any real proper4r in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest of
$2,000 or more (Section 87103(b); Regulation 18703.2);
. 
Any source of income, includingpromised income, which aggregates to $500 or
more within 12 months prior to the decision (Section 87103(c); Regulation
18703.3);
. 
Any source of Stfts to the public official if the gifts aggregate to $320 or mole
within 12 months prior to the decision (Section 87103(e); Regulation 18703.4)
The public official's own personal expenses, income, assets, or liabilities, as well
as those of his or her immediate family-this is known as the "personal financial
effects" rule (Section 87103; Regulation 18703.5).
a
3 An indirect investrnent or interest means any investrnent or interest owned by the spouse of an official or
by a member of the official's immediate family, by an agent on behalf of a public oificial, or by a business
entity or trust in which the official, the official's immediate family, or their agents own directly, indirectly,
or beneficially a l0-percent interest or greater. (Section 87103.) "Immediate family" is defined at Section
82029 as an official's spouse and dependent children.


File No. I-00-277
Page No. 3
Since FFC is a nonprofit organization, it is not a "business entity" as defined in
Section 82005. Thus FFC is not a disqualiffing financial interest for purposes of
Sections 87103(a) and (d). (Eymil Advice Letter, No. I-93-177.) Furthermore, FCC will
not be a source of income to you since you serve as a non-salaried member of its Board
of Directors. Therefore, you do not have a financial interest that may be affected by any
of decisions of the Youthful Offender Parole Board.
fhe wording of your letter suggests that you are also concerned about whether
service on the FFC Board is incompatible with your role as Executive Officer of the
YOPB. The Commission has no jurisdiction in the area of incompatible activities as that
prohibition is not in the Act. Government Code Section 19990 states the general
prohibitions applicable to state officers and employees, and each agency adopts more
specific proscriptions in its Statement of Incompatible Activities. I would direct you to
your agency's general counsel to answer that particular question.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Acting
Counsel
By
Krause
Legal Division
MKjg
